Excellent! No old assertion strings remain. Let me create a summary of the changes made:
## Summary
I have successfully fixed all four test assertion strings in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/shared/github/test_metadata_schemas.py`.
**Changes Made:**
1. **Line 138** (TestWorktreeCreationSchema::test_invalid_issue_number_type): - Changed: `"issue_number must be an integer"` → `"pr_number must be an integer"`
2. **Line 150** (TestWorktreeCreationSchema::test_negative_issue_number): - Changed: `"issue_number must be positive"` → `"pr_number must be positive"`
3. **Line 210** (TestPlanSchema::test_invalid_issue_number_type): - Changed: `"issue_number must be an integer"` → `"pr_number must be an integer"`
4. **Line 221** (TestPlanSchema::test_zero_issue_number): - Changed: `"issue_number must be positive"` → `"pr_number must be positive"`
**What Was NOT Changed:** - Test function names remain unchanged (test_invalid_issue_number_type, test_negative_issue_number, test_zero_issue_number) - Class names remain unchanged (TestWorktreeCreationSchema, TestPlanSchema) - Docstrings remain unchanged - Variable names remain unchanged (issue_number in data dict, worktree_name, etc.) - Only the assertion expected strings in `pytest.raises(ValueError, match="...")` were modified
The changes align the test assertions with the updated validation error messages from the source code, which changed from `issue_number` to `pr_number` in the validation error messages.
